meta.title: Inalpha — Quant agents that evolve under audit.
meta.description: Open-source quant agent framework: factor lab, risk engine, strategy evolution, audit-grade plan/exec. Engineering discipline borrowed from Claude Code.

hero.title: Quant agents
hero.sub: Factor lab · Risk engine · Strategy evolution · Plan/Exec — every factor proposed, every strategy mutated, every order routed is logged, versioned, and reviewable. The LLM writes the code; the engineering harness signs every decision.

problem.title: LLM-written strategies are easy.
problem.sub: Most "LLM + quant" stacks today: prompt in, "long BTC, conf 0.62" out, broker SDK next door. Lost money — no way to find which prompt or which slice of data broke. Made money — no way to tell alpha from overfit. Inalpha brings the Claude Code engineering harness (hooks / permissions / plan-exec) to LLM quant — the LLM only writes code; every decision is signed, every backtest is replayable, and the order path is never reachable by the LLM directly.

lineage.blackbox: "long BTC, conf 0.62" — source data, prompt, model version: nowhere on record. Nothing to attribute when it loses
lineage.inalpha: research_id → backtest_run_id → plan_id, every decision traceable back through its full UUID chain

replay.blackbox: How was last week's sharpe 2.14 computed? Params forgotten, data slice forgotten — recompute from memory
replay.inalpha: strategy_id + params_hash + as_of as a fingerprint. Same numbers any day, any week

guardrail.blackbox: The LLM holds the broker SDK directly. One jailbreak prompt, one hallucination = real money out
guardrail.inalpha: permissions.yaml deny + plan/exec one-shot TTL token. The order path is unreachable by the LLM, ever

thesis.title: Two non-negotiables.

items: Factor / risk / evolution agents, each with its own toolset
Opposing stances in research debate by design
columnA.footer: Not just an LLM call wrapped in a UI.

columnB.header: B. Engineering discipline is the floor
items: Plan → approve → execute with one-shot token
Decision records on disk, declarative configs in repo

thesis.conclusion: A alone is just a chat wrapper. B alone is just another backtest framework. Both — that's Inalpha.

demo.title: Watch them disagree in real time.
demo.blurb: Prompt in. Four agents with opposing stances, distinct toolsets, separate data. Output is a structured decision record — not a paragraph.

btc.label: BTC / FOMC
btc.prompt: Should we long BTC into the FOMC print?

tsla.label: TSLA / Earnings
tsla.prompt: Short TSLA into earnings — fade the rally?

usdjpy.label: USDJPY
usdjpy.prompt: Is USDJPY 158 a BOJ intervention zone?

spyskew.label: SPY skew
spyskew.prompt: SPY put-call skew steep — buy puts?

kernel.title: One codebase.
kernel.sub: Strategy code is written once. Swap the Clock for backtest vs live; swap the Gateway for paper vs broker. Business logic stays put — divergence can only come from physical reality (slippage, latency, data precision).

data.title: data
data.desc: Multi-venue feeds. Freshness-anchored. Same client across markets.

paper.title: paper
paper.desc: In-memory matching, backtest engine, persistent paper trading. State is replay-able.

research.title: research
research.desc: Multi-analyst LLM debate. Opposing stances. No stale numbers passed as insight.

harness.title: Inspired by Claude Code.
harness.sub: Declarative configs decide what each agent can call. Plan → approve → execute is enforced by a one-shot, TTL-bound token — the LLM never reaches the order path unsupervised. Subagents isolate risk. MCP plugs in tools without hand-rolled glue.
harness.hint: ── Click a mechanism on the right to inspect real source

chipDescs.permissions: Tool access scoped per agent role.
chipDescs.hooks: Middleware runs on every tool call.
chipDescs.plan-exec: Plan first, execute once, no runaway loops.
chipDescs.subagent: Risk and review live in isolated subagents.
chipDescs.mcp: Model Context Protocol native, no glue code.

samples.permissions:
defaultMode: ask           # fail-closed

allow:
  - "data.*"               # quotes / news / macro (read)
  - "paper.run_backtest"
  - "research.*"
  - "trade.create_plan"    # proposing is fine
  - "trade.approve_plan"
  - "trade.execute_plan"

deny:                        # the LLM cannot bypass these
  - "paper.submit_order"   # legacy direct-order path
  - "live.submit_order(notional>=10000)"
  - "live.emergency_stop_all"
samples.plan-exec:
$ trade.create_plan
    intent=open_long   symbol=BTCUSDT   side=buy   qty=0.05
    rationale="4h breakout · bull thesis holds"
    researchId=rs_8f3a   backtestRunId=bt_91c2     # ← lineage
→ planId=pln_7c91   status=pending   expireAt=+300s

$ trade.approve_plan { planId, reason="risk gate pass" }
→ approvalToken=tk_…   one-shot · 60s TTL

$ trade.execute_plan { planId, approvalToken }
→ ok · orderId=ord_4d2a   token consumed
samples.swarm:
$ swarm.run_backtest_grid
    candidateIds: [c_3a1, c_7c9, c_5e2]   # D-9 LLM-authored
    symbols:      [BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT]
    venue: binance   timeframe: 1h
    from: 2025-01-01    to: 2026-05-26

→ grid:     3 × 3 = 9 runs    (cap ≤ 20 by grid-size-cap hook)
→ workers:  6   wall: 4.2s    ok: 9 / errored: 0
→ pareto:   3 points (Sharpe ↑  · maxDD ↓)
→ top1:     c_3a1 on ETHUSDT   fitness=1.84
            baseline(buy_and_hold)=0.92  ·  alpha ✓

coverage.title: Same kernel. Same prompts. Same agents.
coverage.sub: All markets route through one orchestrator. Add a venue, every agent gets it for free.

currentState.title: Where we are honest with you
items: D-9 in flight: LLM-authored strategies (E1 MVP) with sandbox gates + multi-objective fitness
RiskEngine rules enforced at the paper HTTP boundary (notional cap / price deviation / drawdown veto)
No real-money deployment yet — every change passes plan/exec approval and lands on disk

cta.title: Star it. Read it. Tear it apart.
cta.sub: Inalpha is alpha-stage and AGPL-3.0. No real money yet — every line is on GitHub.

footer.tagline: Quant agents that evolve under audit.
